required_edits: Rewrite the read-only facade so `forEach` invokes callbacks with the facade—not the private native `Set`—as the third argument, and ensure no other method exposes the backing set. Add mutation-through-callback regression tests for all three registries.

Implement the exact v12 node-reference schema in `validateEdgeInput`; derive each node ID from `canonicalJson({kind, locator})`, accept correctly matching IDs, and reject mismatches. Preserve exact endpoint and caller-owned-field rules.

Apply the frozen exact-key and inherited-enumerable rejection rules at every structured public-input boundary, including `deriveNodeId` and `docAddressKey`, and enforce the exact v12 identifier grammars for all identifier-bearing fields and assertor suffixes.

Replace permissive `trim()`/blank-line filtering in `deriveRepositoryRef` with parsing that accepts only the frozen command-output forms, while retaining the stable shallow-repository error. Add injected tests for whitespace, extra blank lines, malformed roots, and the exact stable error identity.
Build and run the real-git fixture through the required existing no-shell wrapper and fixture-only command allowlist; do not broaden the weaver-facing allowlist.

Complete the test suite to satisfy every frozen coverage item, especially per-kind missing repository/content fields, exact outer schemas and inherited fields, matching and mismatched node IDs, every allowed endpoint row and required forbidden cases, all initial status/assertor combinations, all specified path and malformed source-ref cases, exact git-output behavior, and iteration/mutation-boundary behavior.

Make weaver membership and the shallow-error implementation private unless the frozen interface explicitly exports them; remove the unauthorized `WEAVER_IDS` and `ShallowRepositoryError` named exports and test behavior through the authorized interfaces.
hard_stops: The registry facades are mutable through `forEach`: its third callback argument is the private native `Set`, so a caller can invoke `add`, `delete`, or `clear` on the backing registry.

`validateEdgeInput` does not implement the required node-ID consistency check. Endpoint descriptors contain only `{kind, locator}`, so matching IDs cannot be accepted and mismatched IDs cannot be detected as required by v12.

Fail-closed schema enforcement is incomplete: outer inputs to `deriveNodeId` and `docAddressKey` accept extra or inherited fields, `requireExactKeys` can miss inherited enumerable fields from `Object.prototype`, and identifier-bearing values such as `check-contract.contract_id` and an empty `model:` suffix are accepted without the frozen identifier grammar.

`deriveRepositoryRef` uses broad `trim()` and blank-line filtering, accepting malformed whitespace and additional blank-line forms rather than requiring the exact shallow and single-root output forms.
The real-git fixture invokes `execFileSync` directly through an unrestricted local runner instead of the required existing no-shell wrapper with fixture-only allowlisting.

The mandatory v12 test contract is incomplete, including no mismatched-node-ID test, no per-kind missing `repository_ref` and content-binding tests, incomplete allowed endpoint coverage, incomplete status-coupling/path/source-ref coverage, and no regression for mutation through the `forEach` callback argument.
The module exports `WEAVER_IDS` and `ShallowRepositoryError`, neither of which is in the frozen Task 2 interface. `WEAVER_IDS` in particular creates an unauthorized fourth public registry.
rationale: The implementation gets several foundations right: initial membership and counts for the three required registries, recursive key sorting and array-order preservation, lowercase content-hash validation, the commit exception, the four declared `depends-on` rows, the two declared `discharges` rows, basic status coupling, SHA-256 derivation, zero runtime dependencies, ESM/stdlib use, and a functioning shallow/full real-git scenario. The deterministic gate only proves that the submitted implementation passes its current checks; it does not cover the explicit frozen acceptance requirements. The backing-Set escape and complete omission of node-ID validation are direct semantic failures, while the fixture, output parsing, public exports, and test omissions violate strict scope and proof requirements. The code therefore cannot be accepted AS-IS but is repairable within Task 2 scope.
